200.description: Returns the status of the server

get.summary: Fetches the configuration for all Guards the user has access to.

post.summary: OpenAI SDK compatible endpoint for Chat Completions
Runs the validations specified in a Guard

get.summary: Fetches the history for a specific Guard execution by using the id for the most recent Call

AsOf.name: asOf
AsOf.description: Used to query for data as it existed at this date and time

OpenAiApiKey.name: x-openai-api-key
OpenAiApiKey.description: A valid OpenAI API Key for calling LLM's on your behalf

cause.description: Used to describe the origin of an error if that original error has meaning to the client.  This field should add specificity to 'message'.

fields.description: Used to identify specific fields in a JSON body that caused the error.  Typically only used for 4xx type responses.  The key should be the json path to the invalid property and the value should be a list of error messages specific to that property.

context.description: Used to identify what part of the request caused the error for non-JSON payloads.

validation-outcome.title: ValidationOutcome
validation-outcome.description: The output from a Guard execution.

callId.description: Foreign key to the most recent Call this resulted from.

rawLlmOutput.description: The raw, unchanged string content from the LLM call.

validatedOutput.description: The validated, and potentially fixed, output from the LLM call after undergoing validation.

reask.description: If validation continuously fails and all allocated reasks are used, this field will contain the final reask that would have been sent to the LLM if additional reasks were available.

validationPassed.description: A boolean to indicate whether or not the LLM output passed validation.  If this is False, the validated_output may be invalid.

error.description: If the validation process raised a handleable exception, this field will contain the error message.

validatorName.description: The class name of the validator.

propertyPath.description: The JSON path to the property which was validated that produced this log.

failureReason.description: The error message indicating why validation failed.

reason.description: The reason validation failed, specific to this chunk.

validation-result.title: ValidationResult
validation-result.description: The output from a single Validator.

id.description: The unique identifier for this Validator.  Often the hub id; e.g. guardrails/regex_match

on.description: A reference to the property this validator should be applied against.  Can be a valid JSON path or a meta-property such as "messages" or "output"

anyOf.description: meta-property
JSON path to property; e.g. $.foo.bar

id.description: The unique identifier for the Guard.

name.description: The name for the Guard.

description.description: A description that concisely states the expected behaviour or purpose of the Guard.

id.description: The unique identifier for this Validator.  Often the hub id; e.g. guardrails/regex_match

on.description: A reference to the property this validator should be applied against.  Can be a valid JSON path or a meta-property such as "messages" or "output"

anyOf.description: meta-property
JSON path to property; e.g. $.foo.bar

definitions.$comment: While no longer an official keyword as it is replaced by $defs, this keyword is retained in the meta-schema to prevent incompatible extensions as it remains in common use.

dependencies.$comment: "dependencies" is no longer a keyword, but schema authors should avoid redefining it to facilitate a smooth transition to "dependentSchemas" and "dependentRequired"

format-annotation.title: Format vocabulary meta-schema for annotation results

id.description: The unique identifier for this Call.  Can be used as an identifier for a specific execution of a Guard.
The unique identifier for this Call.  Can be used as an identifier for a specific execution of a Guard.

index.description: The zero-based index of this iteration within the current Call.

callId.description: The unique identifier for the Call that this iteration is a part of.

llmApi.description: The LLM resource targeted by the user. e.g. openai.chat.completions.create

llmOutput.description: The string output from an external LLM call provided by the user via Guard.parse.

promptParams.description: Parameters to be formatted into the messages.

numReasks.description: The total number of times the LLM can be called to correct output excluding the initial call.

metadata.description: Additional data to be used by Validators during execution time.

fullSchemaReask.description: Whether to perform reasks for the entire schema rather than for individual fields.

rawOutput.description: The string content from the LLM response.

error.description: The error message from any exception which interrupted the Guard execution process.

validatorName.description: The class name of the validator.

registeredName.description: The registry id of the validator.

instanceId.description: A unique identifier for the validator that produced this log within the session.

propertyPath.description: The JSON path to the property which was validated that produced this log.

llmOutput.description: The LLM output as a string or the input prompts for the LLM

numReasks.description: An override for the number of re-asks to perform

anyOf.description:
Set of 16 key-value pairs that can be attached to an object. This can be
useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured
format, and querying for objects via API or the dashboard.

Keys are strings with a maximum length of 64 characters. Values are strings
with a maximum length of 512 characters.

An integer between 0 and 20 specifying the number of most likely tokens to
return at each token position, each with an associated log probability.

What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic.
We generally recommend altering this or `top_p` but not both.

An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling,
where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability
mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass
are considered.

We generally recommend altering this or `temperature` but not both.

user.description:
This field is being replaced by `safety_identifier` and `prompt_cache_key`. Use `prompt_cache_key` instead to maintain caching optimizations.
A stable identifier for your end-users.
Used to boost cache hit rates by better bucketing similar requests and  to help OpenAI detect and prevent abuse. [Learn more](/docs/guides/safety-best-practices#safety-identifiers).

safety_identifier.description:
A stable identifier used to help detect users of your application that may be violating OpenAI's usage policies.
The IDs should be a string that uniquely identifies each user, with a maximum length of 64 characters. We recommend hashing their username or email address, in order to avoid sending us any identifying information. [Learn more](/docs/guides/safety-best-practices#safety-identifiers).

prompt_cache_key.description: Used by OpenAI to cache responses for similar requests to optimize your cache hit rates. Replaces the `user` field. [Learn more](/docs/guides/prompt-caching).

anyOf.description:
Specifies the processing type used for serving the request.
  - If set to 'auto', then the request will be processed with the service tier configured in the Project settings. Unless otherwise configured, the Project will use 'default'.
  - If set to 'default', then the request will be processed with the standard pricing and performance for the selected model.
  - If set to '[flex](/docs/guides/flex-processing)' or '[priority](https://openai.com/api-priority-processing/)', then the request will be processed with the corresponding service tier.
  - When not set, the default behavior is 'auto'.

  When the `service_tier` parameter is set, the response body will include the `service_tier` value based on the processing mode actually used to serve the request. This response value may be different from the value set in the parameter.

The retention policy for the prompt cache. Set to `24h` to enable extended prompt caching, which keeps cached prefixes active for longer, up to a maximum of 24 hours. [Learn more](/docs/guides/prompt-caching#prompt-cache-retention).

top_logprobs.description:
An integer between 0 and 20 specifying the number of most likely tokens to
return at each token position, each with an associated log probability.

messages.description:
A list of messages comprising the conversation so far. Depending on the
[model](/docs/models) you use, different message types (modalities) are
supported, like [text](/docs/guides/text-generation),
[images](/docs/guides/vision), and [audio](/docs/guides/audio).

oneOf.title: Developer message
oneOf.description:
Developer-provided instructions that the model should follow, regardless of
messages sent by the user. With o1 models and newer, `developer` messages
replace the previous `system` messages.

content.description: The contents of the developer message.

oneOf.description: The contents of the developer message.
oneOf.title: Text content

oneOf.description: An array of content parts with a defined type. For developer messages, only type `text` is supported.
oneOf.title: Array of content parts

type.description: The type of the content part.

role.description: The role of the messages author, in this case `developer`.

name.description: An optional name for the participant. Provides the model information to differentiate between participants of the same role.

oneOf.title: System message
oneOf.description:
Developer-provided instructions that the model should follow, regardless of
messages sent by the user. With o1 models and newer, use `developer` messages
for this purpose instead.

content.description: The contents of the system message.

oneOf.description: The contents of the system message.
oneOf.title: Text content

oneOf.description: An array of content parts with a defined type. For system messages, only type `text` is supported.
oneOf.title: Array of content parts
Text content part
oneOf.description: Learn about [text inputs](/docs/guides/text-generation).

type.description: The type of the content part.

role.description: The role of the messages author, in this case `system`.

name.description: An optional name for the participant. Provides the model information to differentiate between participants of the same role.

oneOf.title: User message
oneOf.description:
Messages sent by an end user, containing prompts or additional context
information.

content.description: The contents of the user message.

oneOf.description: The text contents of the message.
oneOf.title: Text content

oneOf.description: An array of content parts with a defined type. Supported options differ based on the [model](/docs/models) being used to generate the response. Can contain text, image, or audio inputs.
oneOf.title: Array of content parts
Text content part
oneOf.description: Learn about [text inputs](/docs/guides/text-generation).

type.description: The type of the content part.

oneOf.title: Image content part
oneOf.description: Learn about [image inputs](/docs/guides/vision).

type.description: The type of the content part.

url.description: Either a URL of the image or the base64 encoded image data.

detail.description: Specifies the detail level of the image. Learn more in the [Vision guide](/docs/guides/vision#low-or-high-fidelity-image-understanding).

oneOf.title: Audio content part
oneOf.description: Learn about [audio inputs](/docs/guides/audio).

type.description: The type of the content part. Always `input_audio`.

format.description: The format of the encoded audio data. Currently supports "wav" and "mp3".

oneOf.title: File content part
oneOf.description: Learn about [file inputs](/docs/guides/text) for text generation.

type.description: The type of the content part. Always `file`.

filename.description:
The name of the file, used when passing the file to the model as a 
string.

file_data.description:
The base64 encoded file data, used when passing the file to the model 
as a string.

file_id.description: The ID of an uploaded file to use as input.

role.description: The role of the messages author, in this case `user`.

name.description: An optional name for the participant. Provides the model information to differentiate between participants of the same role.

oneOf.title: Assistant message
oneOf.description: Messages sent by the model in response to user messages.
The contents of the assistant message.
oneOf.title: Text content

oneOf.description: An array of content parts with a defined type. Can be one or more of type `text`, or exactly one of type `refusal`.
oneOf.title: Array of content parts
Text content part
oneOf.description: Learn about [text inputs](/docs/guides/text-generation).

type.description: The type of the content part.
The type of the content part.

refusal.description: The refusal message generated by the model.

anyOf.description: The contents of the assistant message. Required unless `tool_calls` or `function_call` is specified.
The refusal message by the assistant.

role.description: The role of the messages author, in this case `assistant`.

name.description: An optional name for the participant. Provides the model information to differentiate between participants of the same role.

anyOf.description:
Data about a previous audio response from the model.
[Learn more](/docs/guides/audio).

id.description: Unique identifier for a previous audio response from the model.

tool_calls.description: The tool calls generated by the model, such as function calls.

oneOf.title: Function tool call
oneOf.description: A call to a function tool created by the model.

id.description: The ID of the tool call.

type.description: The type of the tool. Currently, only `function` is supported.

function.description: The function that the model called.

name.description: The name of the function to call.

arguments.description: The arguments to call the function with, as generated by the model in JSON format. Note that the model does not always generate valid JSON, and may hallucinate parameters not defined by your function schema. Validate the arguments in your code before calling your function.

oneOf.title: Custom tool call
oneOf.description: A call to a custom tool created by the model.

id.description: The ID of the tool call.

type.description: The type of the tool. Always `custom`.

custom.description: The custom tool that the model called.

name.description: The name of the custom tool to call.

input.description: The input for the custom tool call generated by the model.

anyOf.description: Deprecated and replaced by `tool_calls`. The name and arguments of a function that should be called, as generated by the model.

arguments.description: The arguments to call the function with, as generated by the model in JSON format. Note that the model does not always generate valid JSON, and may hallucinate parameters not defined by your function schema. Validate the arguments in your code before calling your function.

name.description: The name of the function to call.

role.description: The role of the messages author, in this case `tool`.

oneOf.description: The contents of the tool message.
oneOf.title: Text content

oneOf.description: An array of content parts with a defined type. For tool messages, only type `text` is supported.
oneOf.title: Array of content parts
Text content part
oneOf.description: Learn about [text inputs](/docs/guides/text-generation).

type.description: The type of the content part.

content.description: The contents of the tool message.

tool_call_id.description: Tool call that this message is responding to.

role.description: The role of the messages author, in this case `function`.

anyOf.description: The contents of the function message.

name.description: The name of the function to call.

anyOf.description:
Output types that you would like the model to generate.
Most models are capable of generating text, which is the default:

`["text"]`

The `gpt-4o-audio-preview` model can also be used to
[generate audio](/docs/guides/audio). To request that this model generate
both text and audio responses, you can use:

`["text", "audio"]`

Constrains the verbosity of the model's response. Lower values will result in
more concise responses, while higher values will result in more verbose responses.
Currently supported values are `low`, `medium`, and `high`.

Constrains effort on reasoning for
[reasoning models](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/reasoning).
Currently supported values are `none`, `minimal`, `low`, `medium`, `high`, and `xhigh`. Reducing
reasoning effort can result in faster responses and fewer tokens used
on reasoning in a response.

- `gpt-5.1` defaults to `none`, which does not perform reasoning. The supported reasoning values for `gpt-5.1` are `none`, `low`, `medium`, and `high`. Tool calls are supported for all reasoning values in gpt-5.1.
- All models before `gpt-5.1` default to `medium` reasoning effort, and do not support `none`.
- The `gpt-5-pro` model defaults to (and only supports) `high` reasoning effort.
- `xhigh` is supported for all models after `gpt-5.1-codex-max`.

max_completion_tokens.description: An upper bound for the number of tokens that can be generated for a completion, including visible output tokens and [reasoning tokens](/docs/guides/reasoning).

frequency_penalty.description:
Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on
their existing frequency in the text so far, decreasing the model's
likelihood to repeat the same line verbatim.

presence_penalty.description:
Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on
whether they appear in the text so far, increasing the model's likelihood
to talk about new topics.

web_search_options.title: Web search
web_search_options.description:
This tool searches the web for relevant results to use in a response.
Learn more about the [web search tool](/docs/guides/tools-web-search?api-mode=chat).

user_location.description: Approximate location parameters for the search.

type.description: The type of location approximation. Always `approximate`.

approximate.title: Web search location
approximate.description: Approximate location parameters for the search.

country.description:
The two-letter 
[ISO country code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1) of the user,
e.g. `US`.

region.description: Free text input for the region of the user, e.g. `California`.

city.description: Free text input for the city of the user, e.g. `San Francisco`.

search_context_size.description:
High level guidance for the amount of context window space to use for the 
search. One of `low`, `medium`, or `high`. `medium` is the default.

top_logprobs.description:
An integer between 0 and 20 specifying the number of most likely tokens to
return at each token position, each with an associated log probability.
`logprobs` must be set to `true` if this parameter is used.

response_format.description:
An object specifying the format that the model must output.

Setting to `{ "type": "json_schema", "json_schema": {...} }` enables
Structured Outputs which ensures the model will match your supplied JSON
schema. Learn more in the [Structured Outputs
guide](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

Setting to `{ "type": "json_object" }` enables the older JSON mode, which
ensures the message the model generates is valid JSON. Using `json_schema`
is preferred for models that support it.

oneOf.title: Text
oneOf.description: Default response format. Used to generate text responses.

type.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `text`.

oneOf.title: JSON schema
oneOf.description:
JSON Schema response format. Used to generate structured JSON responses.
Learn more about [Structured Outputs](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

type.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `json_schema`.

json_schema.title: JSON schema
json_schema.description: Structured Outputs configuration options, including a JSON Schema.

description.description:
A description of what the response format is for, used by the model to
determine how to respond in the format.

name.description:
The name of the response format. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain
underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

schema.title: JSON schema
schema.description:
The schema for the response format, described as a JSON Schema object.
Learn how to build JSON schemas [here](https://json-schema.org/).

anyOf.description:
Whether to enable strict schema adherence when generating the output.
If set to true, the model will always follow the exact schema defined
in the `schema` field. Only a subset of JSON Schema is supported when
`strict` is `true`. To learn more, read the [Structured Outputs
guide](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

oneOf.title: JSON object
oneOf.description:
JSON object response format. An older method of generating JSON responses.
Using `json_schema` is recommended for models that support it. Note that the
model will not generate JSON without a system or user message instructing it
to do so.

type.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `json_object`.

audio.description:
Parameters for audio output. Required when audio output is requested with
`modalities: ["audio"]`. [Learn more](/docs/guides/audio).

voice.title: Voice
voice.description: A built-in voice name or a custom voice reference.

anyOf.description: Custom voice reference.

format.description:
Specifies the output audio format. Must be one of `wav`, `mp3`, `flac`,
`opus`, or `pcm16`.

store.description:
Whether or not to store the output of this chat completion request for
use in our [model distillation](/docs/guides/distillation) or
[evals](/docs/guides/evals) products.

Supports text and image inputs. Note: image inputs over 8MB will be dropped.

stream.description:
If set to true, the model response data will be streamed to the client
as it is generated using [server-sent events](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events/Using_server-sent_events#Event_stream_format).
See the [Streaming section below](/docs/api-reference/chat/streaming)
for more information, along with the [streaming responses](/docs/guides/streaming-responses)
guide for more information on how to handle the streaming events.

stop.description:
Not supported with latest reasoning models `o3` and `o4-mini`.

Up to 4 sequences where the API will stop generating further tokens. The
returned text will not contain the stop sequence.

logit_bias.description:
Modify the likelihood of specified tokens appearing in the completion.

Accepts a JSON object that maps tokens (specified by their token ID in the
tokenizer) to an associated bias value from -100 to 100. Mathematically,
the bias is added to the logits generated by the model prior to sampling.
The exact effect will vary per model, but values between -1 and 1 should
decrease or increase likelihood of selection; values like -100 or 100
should result in a ban or exclusive selection of the relevant token.

logprobs.description:
Whether to return log probabilities of the output tokens or not. If true,
returns the log probabilities of each output token returned in the
`content` of `message`.

max_tokens.description:
The maximum number of [tokens](/tokenizer) that can be generated in the
chat completion. This value can be used to control
[costs](https://openai.com/api/pricing/) for text generated via API.

This value is now deprecated in favor of `max_completion_tokens`, and is
not compatible with [o-series models](/docs/guides/reasoning).

n.description: How many chat completion choices to generate for each input message. Note that you will be charged based on the number of generated tokens across all of the choices. Keep `n` as `1` to minimize costs.

prediction.description:
Configuration for a [Predicted Output](/docs/guides/predicted-outputs),
which can greatly improve response times when large parts of the model
response are known ahead of time. This is most common when you are
regenerating a file with only minor changes to most of the content.

oneOf.title: Static Content
oneOf.description:
Static predicted output content, such as the content of a text file that is
being regenerated.

type.description:
The type of the predicted content you want to provide. This type is
currently always `content`.

content.description:
The content that should be matched when generating a model response.
If generated tokens would match this content, the entire model response
can be returned much more quickly.

oneOf.title: Text content
oneOf.description:
The content used for a Predicted Output. This is often the
text of a file you are regenerating with minor changes.
An array of content parts with a defined type. Supported options differ based on the [model](/docs/models) being used to generate the response. Can contain text inputs.
oneOf.title: Array of content parts

type.description: The type of the content part.

seed.description:
This feature is in Beta.
If specified, our system will make a best effort to sample deterministically, such that repeated requests with the same `seed` and parameters should return the same result.
Determinism is not guaranteed, and you should refer to the `system_fingerprint` response parameter to monitor changes in the backend.

anyOf.description: Options for streaming response. Only set this when you set `stream: true`.

include_usage.description:
If set, an additional chunk will be streamed before the `data: [DONE]`
message. The `usage` field on this chunk shows the token usage statistics
for the entire request, and the `choices` field will always be an empty
array.

All other chunks will also include a `usage` field, but with a null
value. **NOTE:** If the stream is interrupted, you may not receive the
final usage chunk which contains the total token usage for the request.

include_obfuscation.description:
When true, stream obfuscation will be enabled. Stream obfuscation adds
random characters to an `obfuscation` field on streaming delta events to
normalize payload sizes as a mitigation to certain side-channel attacks.
These obfuscation fields are included by default, but add a small amount
of overhead to the data stream. You can set `include_obfuscation` to
false to optimize for bandwidth if you trust the network links between
your application and the OpenAI API.

tools.description:
A list of tools the model may call. You can provide either
[custom tools](/docs/guides/function-calling#custom-tools) or
[function tools](/docs/guides/function-calling).

oneOf.title: Function tool
oneOf.description: A function tool that can be used to generate a response.

type.description: The type of the tool. Currently, only `function` is supported.

description.description: A description of what the function does, used by the model to choose when and how to call the function.

name.description: The name of the function to be called. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

parameters.description:
The parameters the functions accepts, described as a JSON Schema object. See the [guide](/docs/guides/function-calling) for examples, and the [JSON Schema reference](https://json-schema.org/understanding-json-schema/) for documentation about the format. 

Omitting `parameters` defines a function with an empty parameter list.

anyOf.description: Whether to enable strict schema adherence when generating the function call. If set to true, the model will follow the exact schema defined in the `parameters` field. Only a subset of JSON Schema is supported when `strict` is `true`. Learn more about Structured Outputs in the [function calling guide](/docs/guides/function-calling).

oneOf.title: Custom tool
oneOf.description: A custom tool that processes input using a specified format.

type.description: The type of the custom tool. Always `custom`.

name.description: The name of the custom tool, used to identify it in tool calls.

description.description: Optional description of the custom tool, used to provide more context.

format.description: The input format for the custom tool. Default is unconstrained text.

oneOf.title: Text format
oneOf.description: Unconstrained free-form text.

type.description: Unconstrained text format. Always `text`.

oneOf.title: Grammar format
oneOf.description: A grammar defined by the user.

type.description: Grammar format. Always `grammar`.

syntax.description: The syntax of the grammar definition. One of `lark` or `regex`.

tool_choice.description:
Controls which (if any) tool is called by the model.
`none` means the model will not call any tool and instead generates a message.
`auto` means the model can pick between generating a message or calling one or more tools.
`required` means the model must call one or more tools.
Specifying a particular tool via `{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "my_function"}}` forces the model to call that tool.

`none` is the default when no tools are present. `auto` is the default if tools are present.

oneOf.title: Tool choice mode
oneOf.description: `none` means the model will not call any tool and instead generates a message. `auto` means the model can pick between generating a message or calling one or more tools. `required` means the model must call one or more tools.

oneOf.title: Allowed tools
oneOf.description: Constrains the tools available to the model to a pre-defined set.

type.description: Allowed tool configuration type. Always `allowed_tools`.

allowed_tools.title: Allowed tools
allowed_tools.description: Constrains the tools available to the model to a pre-defined set.

mode.description:
Constrains the tools available to the model to a pre-defined set.

`auto` allows the model to pick from among the allowed tools and generate a
message.

`required` requires the model to call one or more of the allowed tools.

tools.description:
A list of tool definitions that the model should be allowed to call.

For the Chat Completions API, the list of tool definitions might look like:
```json
[
  { "type": "function", "function": { "name": "get_weather" } },
  { "type": "function", "function": { "name": "get_time" } }
]
```

items.description: A tool definition that the model should be allowed to call.

oneOf.title: Function tool choice
oneOf.description: Specifies a tool the model should use. Use to force the model to call a specific function.

type.description: For function calling, the type is always `function`.

name.description: The name of the function to call.

oneOf.title: Custom tool choice
oneOf.description: Specifies a tool the model should use. Use to force the model to call a specific custom tool.

type.description: For custom tool calling, the type is always `custom`.

name.description: The name of the custom tool to call.

parallel_tool_calls.description: Whether to enable [parallel function calling](/docs/guides/function-calling#configuring-parallel-function-calling) during tool use.

function_call.description:
Deprecated in favor of `tool_choice`.

Controls which (if any) function is called by the model.

`none` means the model will not call a function and instead generates a
message.

`auto` means the model can pick between generating a message or calling a
function.

Specifying a particular function via `{"name": "my_function"}` forces the
model to call that function.

`none` is the default when no functions are present. `auto` is the default
if functions are present.

oneOf.description: `none` means the model will not call a function and instead generates a message. `auto` means the model can pick between generating a message or calling a function.
Specifying a particular function via `{"name": "my_function"}` forces the model to call that function.

name.description: The name of the function to call.

functions.description:
Deprecated in favor of `tools`.

A list of functions the model may generate JSON inputs for.

description.description: A description of what the function does, used by the model to choose when and how to call the function.

name.description: The name of the function to be called. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

parameters.description:
The parameters the functions accepts, described as a JSON Schema object. See the [guide](/docs/guides/function-calling) for examples, and the [JSON Schema reference](https://json-schema.org/understanding-json-schema/) for documentation about the format. 

Omitting `parameters` defines a function with an empty parameter list.

CreateChatCompletionResponse.description: Represents a chat completion response returned by model, based on the provided input.

id.description: A unique identifier for the chat completion.

choices.description: A list of chat completion choices. Can be more than one if `n` is greater than 1.

finish_reason.description:
The reason the model stopped generating tokens. This will be `stop` if the model hit a natural stop point or a provided stop sequence,
`length` if the maximum number of tokens specified in the request was reached,
`content_filter` if content was omitted due to a flag from our content filters,
`tool_calls` if the model called a tool, or `function_call` (deprecated) if the model called a function.

index.description: The index of the choice in the list of choices.

message.description: A chat completion message generated by the model.

anyOf.description: The contents of the message.
The refusal message generated by the model.

tool_calls.description: The tool calls generated by the model, such as function calls.

oneOf.title: Function tool call
oneOf.description: A call to a function tool created by the model.

id.description: The ID of the tool call.

type.description: The type of the tool. Currently, only `function` is supported.

function.description: The function that the model called.

name.description: The name of the function to call.

arguments.description: The arguments to call the function with, as generated by the model in JSON format. Note that the model does not always generate valid JSON, and may hallucinate parameters not defined by your function schema. Validate the arguments in your code before calling your function.

oneOf.title: Custom tool call
oneOf.description: A call to a custom tool created by the model.

id.description: The ID of the tool call.

type.description: The type of the tool. Always `custom`.

custom.description: The custom tool that the model called.

name.description: The name of the custom tool to call.

input.description: The input for the custom tool call generated by the model.

annotations.description:
Annotations for the message, when applicable, as when using the
[web search tool](/docs/guides/tools-web-search?api-mode=chat).

items.description: A URL citation when using web search.

type.description: The type of the URL citation. Always `url_citation`.

url_citation.description: A URL citation when using web search.

end_index.description: The index of the last character of the URL citation in the message.

start_index.description: The index of the first character of the URL citation in the message.

url.description: The URL of the web resource.

title.description: The title of the web resource.

role.description: The role of the author of this message.

function_call.description: Deprecated and replaced by `tool_calls`. The name and arguments of a function that should be called, as generated by the model.

arguments.description: The arguments to call the function with, as generated by the model in JSON format. Note that the model does not always generate valid JSON, and may hallucinate parameters not defined by your function schema. Validate the arguments in your code before calling your function.

name.description: The name of the function to call.

anyOf.description:
If the audio output modality is requested, this object contains data
about the audio response from the model. [Learn more](/docs/guides/audio).

id.description: Unique identifier for this audio response.

expires_at.description:
The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when this audio response will
no longer be accessible on the server for use in multi-turn
conversations.

data.description:
Base64 encoded audio bytes generated by the model, in the format
specified in the request.

transcript.description: Transcript of the audio generated by the model.

anyOf.description: Log probability information for the choice.
A list of message content tokens with log probability information.

logprob.description: The log probability of this token, if it is within the top 20 most likely tokens. Otherwise, the value `-9999.0` is used to signify that the token is very unlikely.

anyOf.description: A list of integers representing the UTF-8 bytes representation of the token. Useful in instances where characters are represented by multiple tokens and their byte representations must be combined to generate the correct text representation. Can be `null` if there is no bytes representation for the token.

top_logprobs.description: List of the most likely tokens and their log probability, at this token position. In rare cases, there may be fewer than the number of requested `top_logprobs` returned.

logprob.description: The log probability of this token, if it is within the top 20 most likely tokens. Otherwise, the value `-9999.0` is used to signify that the token is very unlikely.

anyOf.description: A list of integers representing the UTF-8 bytes representation of the token. Useful in instances where characters are represented by multiple tokens and their byte representations must be combined to generate the correct text representation. Can be `null` if there is no bytes representation for the token.
A list of message refusal tokens with log probability information.

logprob.description: The log probability of this token, if it is within the top 20 most likely tokens. Otherwise, the value `-9999.0` is used to signify that the token is very unlikely.

anyOf.description: A list of integers representing the UTF-8 bytes representation of the token. Useful in instances where characters are represented by multiple tokens and their byte representations must be combined to generate the correct text representation. Can be `null` if there is no bytes representation for the token.

top_logprobs.description: List of the most likely tokens and their log probability, at this token position. In rare cases, there may be fewer than the number of requested `top_logprobs` returned.

logprob.description: The log probability of this token, if it is within the top 20 most likely tokens. Otherwise, the value `-9999.0` is used to signify that the token is very unlikely.

anyOf.description: A list of integers representing the UTF-8 bytes representation of the token. Useful in instances where characters are represented by multiple tokens and their byte representations must be combined to generate the correct text representation. Can be `null` if there is no bytes representation for the token.

created.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) of when the chat completion was created.

model.description: The model used for the chat completion.

anyOf.description:
Specifies the processing type used for serving the request.
  - If set to 'auto', then the request will be processed with the service tier configured in the Project settings. Unless otherwise configured, the Project will use 'default'.
  - If set to 'default', then the request will be processed with the standard pricing and performance for the selected model.
  - If set to '[flex](/docs/guides/flex-processing)' or '[priority](https://openai.com/api-priority-processing/)', then the request will be processed with the corresponding service tier.
  - When not set, the default behavior is 'auto'.

  When the `service_tier` parameter is set, the response body will include the `service_tier` value based on the processing mode actually used to serve the request. This response value may be different from the value set in the parameter.

system_fingerprint.description:
This fingerprint represents the backend configuration that the model runs with.

Can be used in conjunction with the `seed` request parameter to understand when backend changes have been made that might impact determinism.

object.description: The object type, which is always `chat.completion`.

usage.description: Usage statistics for the completion request.

completion_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the generated completion.

prompt_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the prompt.

total_tokens.description: Total number of tokens used in the request (prompt + completion).

completion_tokens_details.description: Breakdown of tokens used in a completion.

accepted_prediction_tokens.description:
When using Predicted Outputs, the number of tokens in the
prediction that appeared in the completion.

audio_tokens.description: Audio input tokens generated by the model.

reasoning_tokens.description: Tokens generated by the model for reasoning.

rejected_prediction_tokens.description:
When using Predicted Outputs, the number of tokens in the
prediction that did not appear in the completion. However, like
reasoning tokens, these tokens are still counted in the total
completion tokens for purposes of billing, output, and context window
limits.

prompt_tokens_details.description: Breakdown of tokens used in the prompt.

audio_tokens.description: Audio input tokens present in the prompt.

cached_tokens.description: Cached tokens present in the prompt.

CreateChatCompletionStreamResponse.description:
Represents a streamed chunk of a chat completion response returned
by the model, based on the provided input. 
[Learn more](/docs/guides/streaming-responses).

id.description: A unique identifier for the chat completion. Each chunk has the same ID.

choices.description:
A list of chat completion choices. Can contain more than one elements if `n` is greater than 1. Can also be empty for the
last chunk if you set `stream_options: {"include_usage": true}`.

delta.description: A chat completion delta generated by streamed model responses.

anyOf.description: The contents of the chunk message.

function_call.description: Deprecated and replaced by `tool_calls`. The name and arguments of a function that should be called, as generated by the model.

arguments.description: The arguments to call the function with, as generated by the model in JSON format. Note that the model does not always generate valid JSON, and may hallucinate parameters not defined by your function schema. Validate the arguments in your code before calling your function.

name.description: The name of the function to call.

id.description: The ID of the tool call.

type.description: The type of the tool. Currently, only `function` is supported.

name.description: The name of the function to call.

arguments.description: The arguments to call the function with, as generated by the model in JSON format. Note that the model does not always generate valid JSON, and may hallucinate parameters not defined by your function schema. Validate the arguments in your code before calling your function.

role.description: The role of the author of this message.

anyOf.description: The refusal message generated by the model.

logprobs.description: Log probability information for the choice.

content.description: A list of message content tokens with log probability information.

logprob.description: The log probability of this token, if it is within the top 20 most likely tokens. Otherwise, the value `-9999.0` is used to signify that the token is very unlikely.

anyOf.description: A list of integers representing the UTF-8 bytes representation of the token. Useful in instances where characters are represented by multiple tokens and their byte representations must be combined to generate the correct text representation. Can be `null` if there is no bytes representation for the token.

top_logprobs.description: List of the most likely tokens and their log probability, at this token position. In rare cases, there may be fewer than the number of requested `top_logprobs` returned.

anyOf.description: A list of integers representing the UTF-8 bytes representation of the token. Useful in instances where characters are represented by multiple tokens and their byte representations must be combined to generate the correct text representation. Can be `null` if there is no bytes representation for the token.

refusal.description: A list of message refusal tokens with log probability information.

logprob.description: The log probability of this token, if it is within the top 20 most likely tokens. Otherwise, the value `-9999.0` is used to signify that the token is very unlikely.

anyOf.description: A list of integers representing the UTF-8 bytes representation of the token. Useful in instances where characters are represented by multiple tokens and their byte representations must be combined to generate the correct text representation. Can be `null` if there is no bytes representation for the token.

top_logprobs.description: List of the most likely tokens and their log probability, at this token position. In rare cases, there may be fewer than the number of requested `top_logprobs` returned.

logprob.description: The log probability of this token, if it is within the top 20 most likely tokens. Otherwise, the value `-9999.0` is used to signify that the token is very unlikely.

anyOf.description: A list of integers representing the UTF-8 bytes representation of the token. Useful in instances where characters are represented by multiple tokens and their byte representations must be combined to generate the correct text representation. Can be `null` if there is no bytes representation for the token.

finish_reason.description:
The reason the model stopped generating tokens. This will be `stop` if the model hit a natural stop point or a provided stop sequence,
`length` if the maximum number of tokens specified in the request was reached,
`content_filter` if content was omitted due to a flag from our content filters,
`tool_calls` if the model called a tool, or `function_call` (deprecated) if the model called a function.

index.description: The index of the choice in the list of choices.

created.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) of when the chat completion was created. Each chunk has the same timestamp.

model.description: The model to generate the completion.

anyOf.description:
Specifies the processing type used for serving the request.
  - If set to 'auto', then the request will be processed with the service tier configured in the Project settings. Unless otherwise configured, the Project will use 'default'.
  - If set to 'default', then the request will be processed with the standard pricing and performance for the selected model.
  - If set to '[flex](/docs/guides/flex-processing)' or '[priority](https://openai.com/api-priority-processing/)', then the request will be processed with the corresponding service tier.
  - When not set, the default behavior is 'auto'.

  When the `service_tier` parameter is set, the response body will include the `service_tier` value based on the processing mode actually used to serve the request. This response value may be different from the value set in the parameter.

This fingerprint represents the backend configuration that the model runs with.
Can be used in conjunction with the `seed` request parameter to understand when backend changes have been made that might impact determinism.
